value:
# Introduction to React in MDX

<Card title="Component State">
State allows components to create, update, and use data internally. In our MDX app, this is very useful!
</Card>

<Admonition title="Warning">
Do not mutate state directly! Always use the setter function provided by `useState`.
</Admonition>

Introduction to React in MDX

...

value:
# Rust Borrow Checker

<Card title="Ownership Rules">
1. Each value in Rust has a variable that’s called its owner.
2. There can only be one owner at a time.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Important">
When the owner goes out of scope, the value will be dropped.
</Admonition>

# Flexbox Basics

<Card title="Flex Container">
Setting `display: flex` creates a block-level flex container, establishing a new flex formatting context for its direct children.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Pro Tip">
Use `align-items: center` to vertically align elements effortlessly.
</Admonition>

# Authentication API Docs

<Card title="Endpoints">
The primary login endpoint is `POST /api/v1/auth/login`. It expects a JSON payload containing `email` and `password`.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Security Context">
Always send JWT tokens via HTTPS and store them securely in HttpOnly cookies to prevent XSS attacks.
</Admonition>

# Indexing Strategy

<Card title="B-Trees">
Most relational databases use B-Trees for default indexing to keep data sorted and allow searches, sequential access, insertions, and deletions in logarithmic time.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Performance Note">
Beware of over-indexing. Too many indexes can significantly slow down write operations (`INSERT`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`).
</Admonition>

# Docker Volumes

<Card title="Persisting Data">
Volumes are the preferred mechanism for persisting data generated by and used by Docker containers. They are completely managed by Docker.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Warning">
Bind mounts depend on the host machine's directory structure, which can cause cross-platform compatibility issues.
</Admonition>

# Git Rebase Interactive

<Card title="Rewriting History">
Rebasing allows you to squash commits, edit commit messages, and reorder history using the `git rebase -i` command.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Danger Zone">
Never rebase commits that have already been pushed to a public or shared repository. You will overwrite history others might be basing their work on!
</Admonition>

# Jest Mocks

<Card title="Mock Functions">
Mock functions allow you to test links between code by erasing the actual implementation of a function, capturing calls, and setting up return values.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Info">
Use `jest.fn()` to create a basic mock, and `jest.spyOn()` if you need to watch an existing object method while optionally maintaining its original behavior.
</Admonition>

# Web Accessibility: ARIA Roles

<Card title="Navigation Role">
The `navigation` landmark role is used for major navigational blocks. Screen readers use these to skip directly to navigation.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Best Practice">
Native HTML elements (like `<nav>`) are always preferred over adding ARIA roles to non-semantic `<div>` elements.
</Admonition>

# TypeScript Utility Types

<Card title="Partial<Type>">
Constructs a type with all properties of `Type` set to optional. This utility returns a type that represents all subsets of a given type.
</Card>

<Admonition title="Note">
`Omit<Type, Keys>` is great for removing sensitive fields (like passwords) from a User interface type before returning it to the client.
</Admonition>

# Introduction to React in MDX

<Card title="Component State">
State allows components to create, update, and use data internally. In our MDX app, this is very useful!
</Card>

<Admonition title="Warning">
Do not mutate state directly! Always use the setter function provided by `useState`.
</Admonition>

<Card title="Component State">
State allows components to create, update, and use data internally. In our MDX app, this is very useful!
</Card>

<Admonition title="Warning">
Do not mutate state directly! Always use the setter function provided by `useState`.
</Admonition>
